name: Garrett Wilson
headline: Healthy Wilson reclaims target hog role with Geno upgrade
body:
A knee injury wrecked Wilson's 2025 after just seven games, but the underlying usage stayed elite while he played: 12.5% target share looks soft until you see he ran routes on barely a third of snaps, yet still drew 0.303 targets per route. The healthy version is the 2024 monster who posted a 26% target share, 0.624 WOPR, 101 grabs for 1,104 yards and a 52.3% success rate at +0.142 EPA per play. He has anchored this passing game for three years running, and three straight seasons north of 150 targets when on the field tells the real story.

The context finally improves. Geno Smith and Frank Reich replace a broken quarterback room, and Wilson remains the clear first read despite added complementary weapons. He's a strong WR2 with WR1 ceiling around an early-Round-4 ADP. Buy the volume; the floor and the upside both point up.
